results.title: 'Screen It' 2015 [winning entries and special mentions]
results.brief_description:
‘Screen It’ was Australia’s national film, animation and game making competition for school-aged students, run by ACMI for 16 years to 2019.

Designed to encourage imagination and inventiveness in primary and secondary school students, ‘Screen It’ fostered a new generation of young media makers. In 2015 budding young film and game-makers took up the challenge of creating their own short film or game in response to the theme “Change”.

Witness first-hand the amazing winning entries of the 2015 Screen It competition!

results.title: The Long Now
results.brief_description: There is a giant clock ringing deep inside a mountain in the belly of the Sierra Diablo Ranges - The land of the Devil. It is a huge clock, hundreds of feet tall, designed to tick for 10,000 years. The mountain in which the clock resides is privately owned by Jeff Bezos. Programmed never to chime the same melody twice, its anyone’s guess how many beautiful songs will never be heard over the clock’s 10 millennial lifespan. Xanthe Dobbie’s new experimental desktop performance ponders the human urge to seek immortality. Featuring original epic hero Gilgamesh and the disembodied deep fake voice of Alan Rickman, The Long Now collapses histories, illuminating ancient and contemporary myth-making practices.

results.title: Spectrum of Brick Lane 2
results.material_description: Lightboxes, steel shelving, acrylic sheet, fluorescent lights, cable and plug boards
results.brief_description: Colour is perceived through light reflected from the object we’re observing. The wavelength of the light determines the hue we see. Batchelor’s illuminated sculpture reminds us of this connection between light and colour, and is inspired by London’s bustling Brick Lane.
results.description: We perceive colour through light reflected from the object we are observing. The wavelength of the light determines the hue we see. Batchelor’s illuminated sculpture reminds us of this connection between light and colour. This work is inspired by the bright windows and signs on Brick Lane, a bustling London street renowned for its South Asian restaurants. Here, light is not connected to spirituality or nature, but is rooted in everyday experiences.
